scope: Sanitized identity, historical Auto-Scan, Gateway Installation List and UI-observation intake for Audi MMI 3G High HNAV baseline HNAV-8T1035664F-K0257-D1.
privacyNotes: No full VIN, plate, customer data, private path, access code, serial number, coding value, WSC, VCID, proprietary URL or binary package is included.

items.name: 5F diagnostic controller identification
items.notes: The 2026-05-12 Auto-Scan directly captures 5F part number, hardware identifier, component and software level for that scan. Engineering-menu firmware train, Variant, RadioUnit and Sample remain PARTIAL.

items.name: Historical full Auto-Scan context
items.notes: A reviewed public-safe summary and extract are attached. The raw full scan remains private and this historical scan does not replace a fresh pre-experiment scan.

items.name: Gateway Installation List reconstructed from photographed VCDS screens
items.requiredBefore: Installed-versus-possible address and infotainment dependency review
items.notes: Three photographed VCDS list pages were identified and a complete address 01-8C transcription exists. This is visual reconstructed evidence, not a native VCDS export. No checkbox or list write is authorized.

items.name: Red Engineering Menu identification
items.notes: Recovered prior-chat readings exist, but original image files and exact menu path are not attached. No update, emergency or SWDL action is authorized.

items.name: Green Menu structure observation
items.notes: Recovered from prior user-supplied screenshot discussions. Visibility does not confirm function.

items.name: Green Menu diagnostic fault observations
items.notes: Recovered display strings are observations only and do not establish root cause.

items.name: Green Menu NADPhone version observation
items.notes: The recovered $ERROR$ display does not prove module presence, failure mode or telephone functionality.

items.name: Historical DTC-before context
items.notes: DTCs for 19, 5F and 6C were extracted and reviewed. A fresh current DTC-before capture remains required before any controlled experiment.

items.name: Auto-Scan sanitization and provenance review
items.notes: Records selection, exclusions, privacy review, source limitations and the distinction between the directly available 265250 km scan and the report-only 268420 km EP-001 scan.

items.name: Original coding backup
items.notes: The Auto-Scan showed that a coding field exists, but the coding value is intentionally not stored in this public intake.

items.name: Read-only adaptation map
items.notes: No adaptation value is included in this intake.

items.name: Dataset and recovery backup
items.requiredBefore: Any dataset, parameterization or SWDL research
items.notes: Binary packages, access material and private recovery assets must not be published in this intake.

blockedClaims: The 5F diagnostic identity applies unchanged outside the 2026-05-12 scan context.
The reconstructed Gateway Installation List is a native VCDS export or proves physical module presence.
An unchecked Installation List address proves that related hardware is physically absent.
The engineering-menu firmware train, Variant, RadioUnit or Sample is confirmed by the Auto-Scan.
Sample d1 is proven to be equivalent to hwSample.
The historical Auto-Scan represents the current pre-experiment state.
Green Menu visibility proves feature availability or functionality.
Green Menu or Auto-Scan DTC strings establish a confirmed root cause.
Coding or adaptation behavior is confirmed.
Security Access is known, valid or required for a specific operation.
Dataset, parameterization, emergency update or SWDL operations are safe.
HNAV to HN+ conversion feasibility or barrier location is established.
notes: The package now contains a directly sourced sanitized historical Auto-Scan summary, DTC extract and a reconstructed visual Gateway Installation List. It remains PARTIAL because a fresh current scan, a native/direct Installation List export or new screenshots, original Red/Green Menu images, coding backup, adaptation map and recovery assets are still missing.
